title: Prefetch, Consumer Concurrency, Capacity, and Fairness · Contract Lens: Prefetch Window Semantics
summary: A consumer sets prefetch zero for maximum speed, starts unbounded promises, ignores message bytes and dependency pools, and scales consumers from queue depth alone. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prefetch window semantics increment in prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain RabbitMQ per-consumer prefetch, deprecated global QoS, in-flight races, quorum caps, and the zero-means-unbounded hazard.
Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Prefetch Window Semantics · Theory Prefetch Window Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 7449 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine prefetch, active handlers, downstream capacity, and message-byte budgets; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Prefetch Window Semantics contract for the supplied data.
